policy_statement: Conduct biannual PSC reviews starting in 2024 of the renewable energy program and resource mix to support emissions targets and evaluate retirement/repurposing of fossil generation; complete a 2023 NYC fossil facility retirement/redevelopment blueprint including specified community and workforce measures.
mechanism_description: The PSC will conduct biannual reviews and develop a retirement/redevelopment blueprint to guide decisions on the resource mix, including retiring or repurposing fossil generation, supporting power-sector emissions reductions while planning for community and worker impacts.
primary_causal_pathway: Electricity system planning + fossil retirements → ↓ fossil electricity generation → ↓ CO₂/CH₄ emissions → ↓ atmospheric forcing
causal_mechanism_detail: (1) Change in human activity/system: Public utility/energy regulators and system planners revise the resource mix through integrated planning, periodic reviews, and formal retirement/redevelopment plans that retire, repower, or repurpose fossil generating units and enable clean replacements. (2) Mediating physical process: Reduced combustion of coal/oil/gas for electricity (and associated upstream methane leakage) decreases mass emissions of CO₂ and CH₄ from the power system. (3) Climate-relevant outcome: Net reduction in power-sector GHG emissions, contributing to slower accumulation of heat-trapping gases in the atmosphere.
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is lowering electricity-sector greenhouse gas emissions by planning and executing fossil generator retirements and replacing them with lower/zero-carbon resources.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: resource planning and retirement decisions reduce fossil generation and therefore directly reduce GHG emissions from electricity supply.  
2) Why this primary over alternatives: while the blueprint includes community and worker measures (a “just transition” component) and can include reliability planning, the core climate effect is emissions mitigation via grid decarbonization, not adaptation, resource efficiency, or nature-based carbon sequestration.  
3) Edge cases applied: if a “retirement plan” only studies options without influencing actual retirements or clean procurement, mitigation impact could be indirect; however, the canonical mechanism explicitly targets retirement/repurposing to support emissions reductions, making mitigation dominant. Reliability/resilience co-benefits do not outweigh the emissions pathway.  
4) Confirm primary applies to ALL instances: any policy under this mechanism (resource planning + fossil retirement planning leading to grid decarbonization) has its main climate-relevant effect through reduced fossil generation emissions.  
5) Secondary subtype deferred: specific subtypes (e.g., coal vs gas retirement, renewable procurement, worker transition funding) depend on policy text and implementation details and are deferred to policy-level evidence.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: If a plan results in replacing retired fossil capacity with other fossil resources (e.g., new gas peakers) or primarily serves reliability without net emissions decline, the mitigation effect could be weaker; those cases would require policy-level evidence but are outside the intended scope of this canonical mechanism.
key_indicators: "PSC will conduct biannual reviews"; "regular and transparent resource planning"; "evaluate options to retire and/or repurpose existing fossil fuel electric generation facilities"; "development of a blueprint... will be completed in 2023"; "analysis and planning to address the impacts on communities and workers"
co_benefits: Just transition co-benefits: community impact mitigation, workforce transition planning/redevelopment support.
instance_edge_case_notes: Instrument is primarily a planning/review and blueprint requirement (enabling), not an explicit enforceable retirement mandate or procurement order in the quoted text. The blueprint and reviews may later translate into regulatory mandates, but that is not specified here.
